title: BSP ctl-spec-caps 0x428 audit
trigger: Vendor A733 SDMMC0 DTS uses ctl-spec-caps=<0x428>, which had not been explicitly decoded in the handoff queue.

decode.0x428: retry + manual read data timeout + CMD11 timeout detect

findings: The 0x428 bits affect retry and timeout handling paths.
These bits do not change descriptor format, descriptor address translation, DMA mask, storage fabric clocks, reset order, GCTRL DMA enable, IDIE, DMAC enable, or the IDMAC descriptor-fetch path.
The current lab failure occurs before descriptor word 0 is consumed: IDST=0x4000 DESC_READ, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, BBCR=0, descriptor OWN unchanged.
Manual read-data timeout logic may affect how a hung read is detected or recovered, but not whether IDMAC can fetch descriptor word 0.

public_search.notes: Search still did not reveal a public archive hit or answer for the manually sent A733 SDMMC0 descriptor-fetch question.

reason: ctl-spec-caps=<0x428> is timeout/retry policy, not a descriptor-fetch precondition. Testing it would only alter hang detection/recovery around the same stuck IDMAC state.
next: Do not retest ctl-spec-caps as a standalone descriptor-fetch fix.
Keep waiting for a maintainer/list reply or a source-backed SDMMC0 firewall/security/master-permission bit.
